channel.title: Morning Defense
channel.description: From the SitRoom to the E-Ring, the inside scoop on defense, national security and foreign policy.

item.title: Five Eyes in focus with Brat nomination
item.description: President Donald Trump’s recently announced nominee for U.S. ambassador to Australia is on track to take up his post at a time of stress on the Five Eyes alliance. That could be awkward given that Dave Brat has a history of disparaging the U.S. intelligence community.
item.dc:creator: By Daniella Cheslow and Giselle Ruhiyyih Ewing
item.dc:contributor: By Daniella Cheslow and Giselle Ruhiyyih Ewing

item.title: Sheinbaum’s Trump dilemma
item.description: Mexican President Claudia Sheinbaum is caught in a conundrum following the Trump administration's indictments of a slew of current and former high-ranking Mexican officials.
item.dc:creator: By Giselle Ruhiyyih Ewing and Daniella Cheslow
item.dc:contributor: By Giselle Ruhiyyih Ewing and Daniella Cheslow

item.title: Price of Iran war: $25B and no easy exits
item.description: The first official price tag of the Iran war is out: $25 billion. And the administration is considering a supplemental request for much more.
item.dc:creator: By Daniella Cheslow and Giselle Ruhiyyih Ewing
item.dc:contributor: By Daniella Cheslow and Giselle Ruhiyyih Ewing

item.title: God save the US-UK relationship
item.dc:creator: By Daniella Cheslow and Giselle Ruhiyyih Ewing
item.dc:contributor: By Daniella Cheslow and Giselle Ruhiyyih Ewing

item.title: Finding the gaps in WHCD security
item.dc:creator: By Daniella Cheslow and Giselle Ruhiyyih Ewing
item.dc:contributor: By Daniella Cheslow and Giselle Ruhiyyih Ewing

item.title: Tokyo hedges against an ‘unpredictable’ US
item.dc:creator: By Phelim Kine and Giselle Ruhiyyih Ewing
item.dc:contributor: By Phelim Kine and Giselle Ruhiyyih Ewing

item.title: Secret chats, sneaky letters surround Cuba talks
item.description: Cuba’s two-week deadline to release political prisoners is coming to a close.
item.dc:creator: By Giselle Ruhiyyih Ewing and Daniella Cheslow
item.dc:contributor: By Giselle Ruhiyyih Ewing and Daniella Cheslow

item.title: NATO workarounds bloom in Europe
item.description: As President Donald Trump has continued to threaten withdrawing from NATO, the number of initiatives in Europe to become less dependent on the alliance is proliferating.
item.dc:creator: By Daniella Cheslow and Giselle Ruhiyyih Ewing
item.dc:contributor: By Daniella Cheslow and Giselle Ruhiyyih Ewing

item.title: ‘Dark shadows’ over Iran talks
item.description: The latest deadline for the U.S. and Iran to strike a deal is coming up before a ceasefire expires tomorrow, and the signs are not looking great for another round of talks in Islamabad.
item.dc:creator: By Daniella Cheslow and Giselle Ruhiyyih Ewing
item.dc:contributor: By Daniella Cheslow and Giselle Ruhiyyih Ewing

item.title: Europe convenes Gaza summits as Board of Peace stalls
item.description: With the Gaza Strip in need of an estimated $71 billion in reconstruction funds, and efforts to disarm Hamas making glacial progress if at all, Europe hosted two parallel meetings today aimed at pushing forward on aid and recovery — and maybe finding new avenues to work with the United States.
item.dc:creator: By Daniella Cheslow and Giselle Ruhiyyih Ewing
item.dc:contributor: By Daniella Cheslow and Giselle Ruhiyyih Ewing

item.title: The Diego Garcia standoff intensifies
item.description: The Trump administration’s refusal to give any ground on the joint U.S.-U.K. Diego Garcia military base in the Indian Ocean has it on a collision course with Mauritius.
item.dc:creator: By Phelim Kine and Daniella Cheslow
item.dc:contributor: By Phelim Kine and Daniella Cheslow

item.title: Mullin's bid to rehab DHS' image
item.description: The Department of Homeland Security is recalibrating its messaging under Secretary Markwayne Mullin.
item.dc:creator: By Eric Bazail-Eimil and Daniella Cheslow
item.dc:contributor: By Eric Bazail-Eimil and Daniella Cheslow

item.title: Your new neighbor, Viktor Orbán?
item.description: Shortly after Hungarian Prime Minister Viktor Orbán’s electoral defeat on Sunday, Hungarian journalist Szabolcs Panyi predicted that the deposed autocrat might seek refuge in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: By Daniella Cheslow and Giselle Ruhiyyih Ewing
item.dc:contributor: By Daniella Cheslow and Giselle Ruhiyyih Ewing

item.title: Dems fly war powers banner without GOP support
item.description: Two weeks of recess appears to have done little to change the math in Congress around restricting President Donald Trump’s power to wage war, even after he threatened to destroy Iran’s civilization.
item.dc:creator: By Daniella Cheslow and Giselle Ruhiyyih Ewing
item.dc:contributor: By Daniella Cheslow and Giselle Ruhiyyih Ewing

item.title: The return of Russia oil sanctions
item.description: U.S. sanctions on Russian oil are back on after the Trump administration let a waiver expire this weekend.
item.dc:creator: By Daniella Cheslow and Giselle Ruhiyyih Ewing
item.dc:contributor: By Daniella Cheslow and Giselle Ruhiyyih Ewing

item.title: DHS can’t stop posting about Spanberger
item.dc:creator: By Eric Bazail-Eimil and Giselle Ruhiyyih Ewing
item.dc:contributor: By Eric Bazail-Eimil and Giselle Ruhiyyih Ewing

item.title: Trump's Iran deal 'optimism' could hit negotiating realities
item.description: The U.S. and Iran have just twelve days to strike a peace deal — an incredibly tight window to hash out a lasting agreement.
item.dc:creator: By Giselle Ruhiyyih Ewing, Eric Bazail-Eimil and Daniella Cheslow
item.dc:contributor: By Giselle Ruhiyyih Ewing, Eric Bazail-Eimil and Daniella Cheslow

item.title: US-Iran deal could hinge on Lebanon
item.description: Amid jubilation over a U.S.-Iran ceasefire, Israel’s army said it struck more than 100 targets in Lebanon in 10 minutes in one of the deadliest days of the war in that country — posing a serious challenge to the truce.
item.dc:creator: By Daniella Cheslow and Giselle Ruhiyyih Ewing
item.dc:contributor: By Daniella Cheslow and Giselle Ruhiyyih Ewing

item.title: Can Pakistan get the US and Iran off the ledge?
item.description: Once a pariah in the Biden administration's Washington, Pakistan is now taking a star turn in the spotlight under the Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: By Daniella Cheslow and Giselle Ruhiyyih Ewing
item.dc:contributor: By Daniella Cheslow and Giselle Ruhiyyih Ewing

item.title: De-escalating Iran war: Many cooks, no soup
item.description: A Pakistani-led proposal for a ceasefire between the U.S. and Iran reportedly ended in rejection.
item.dc:creator: By Daniella Cheslow and Giselle Ruhiyyih Ewing
item.dc:contributor: By Daniella Cheslow and Giselle Ruhiyyih Ewing

item.title: Amid Iran war, Syria’s Al-Sharaa charts new path
item.dc:creator: By Daniella Cheslow and Giselle Ruhiyyih Ewing
item.dc:contributor: By Daniella Cheslow and Giselle Ruhiyyih Ewing

item.title: Iran war puts Cuba on the back burner
item.description: With Tehran dominating the agenda, Havana is getting a reprieve — for now.
item.dc:creator: By Giselle Ruhiyyih Ewing, Eric Bazail-Eimil and Daniella Cheslow
item.dc:contributor: By Giselle Ruhiyyih Ewing, Eric Bazail-Eimil and Daniella Cheslow

item.title: Iran war squeezes African countries
item.description: Missiles over Dubai and bombs in Middle East oil fields have captured much of the attention of the last month of the U.S.-Israeli strikes on Iran. But Africa is feeling the pain acutely, if off the global radar.
item.dc:creator: By Daniella Cheslow and Giselle Ruhiyyih Ewing
item.dc:contributor: By Daniella Cheslow and Giselle Ruhiyyih Ewing

item.title: Trump admin resurrects soft power
item.description: When President Donald Trump swept back into office last year, he made a point of dismantling key pillars of the nation’s soft power apparatus.
item.dc:creator: By Daniella Cheslow and Giselle Ruhiyyih Ewing
item.dc:contributor: By Daniella Cheslow and Giselle Ruhiyyih Ewing

item.title: Iran flashes its Houthi card
item.description: Iran has a warning for the U.S. as thousands of Marines arrive in the Middle East: it can make the standoff over the Strait of Hormuz even more painful, including through its proxy Houthi militias in Yemen.
item.dc:creator: By Daniella Cheslow and Giselle Ruhiyyih Ewing
item.dc:contributor: By Daniella Cheslow and Giselle Ruhiyyih Ewing

item.title: New York Dems tussle over West Bank response
item.dc:creator: By Daniella Cheslow and Giselle Ruhiyyih Ewing
item.dc:contributor: By Daniella Cheslow and Giselle Ruhiyyih Ewing

item.title: Robbing Kyiv to fight Tehran
item.description: As the Iran war approaches the four-week mark, the strain it's putting on the Ukraine war a continent away is becoming increasingly evident.
item.dc:creator: By Giselle Ruhiyyih Ewing and Daniella Cheslow
item.dc:contributor: By Giselle Ruhiyyih Ewing and Daniella Cheslow

item.title: Joe Kent ‘huge’ in Tehran, small in DC
item.description: Iranian observers saw Joe Kent's resignation from the Trump administration last week as the first defection over the war, and as a sign of fragility in the MAGA base.
item.dc:creator: By Daniella Cheslow and Giselle Ruhiyyih Ewing
item.dc:contributor: By Daniella Cheslow and Giselle Ruhiyyih Ewing

item.title: Iran standoff puts Taiwan chips at risk
item.description: Taiwan is scrambling to prevent its semiconductor sector from becoming a casualty of the Iran war.
item.dc:creator: By Phelim Kine, Daniella Cheslow and Giselle Ruhiyyih Ewing
item.dc:contributor: By Phelim Kine, Daniella Cheslow and Giselle Ruhiyyih Ewing

item.title: Trump touts Iran talks. Tehran says no dice.
item.description: Iran and the U.S. are sending mixed signals about what kind of diplomacy they’re doing — if at all.
item.dc:creator: By Daniella Cheslow and Giselle Ruhiyyih Ewing
item.dc:contributor: By Daniella Cheslow and Giselle Ruhiyyih Ewing
